hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Reduce PNG file size while maintaining transparency and quality. Perfect for logos, icons, and graphics.

dropzone.orClick: or click to browse PNG files
dropzone.maxSize: Max 5MB per file - Up to 10 PNG images per upload

qualitySettings.title: Quality Settings
qualitySettings.description: PNG compression is lossless. Lower quality settings will still maintain visual quality but reduce file size by optimizing the compression algorithm.

privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: Your images are processed on-the-fly. Originals stay in your browser; compressed results are streamed back immediately.
privacy.p2: No permanent storage: the server keeps temporary buffers just long enough to encode and deliver the result, then memory is released.
privacy.p3: No analytics on image content; only anonymous aggregate performance counters may be collected (sizes, timings) for optimization.
privacy.p4: Close the tab any time after processing completes. While processing, avoid closing to prevent interruption.
privacy.notice: All processing local + server-side transient, no files stored.

status.settingsChangedDesc: Click Apply New Settings to re-compress your PNG images with the new quality configuration.

howTo.title: How To Compress PNG
howTo.step1Desc: Drag and drop your graphics or icons. We fully support transparent alpha channels.
howTo.step2Desc: Adjust the quality slider to find the perfect efficiency ratio without destroying pixel fidelity.
howTo.step3Desc: Get your bandwidth-friendly PNGs back immediately. Perfect for crisp web UI elements.

makeSmaller.title: Make Your PNG Smaller Without Losing Quality
makeSmaller.card1Desc: Compress PNG files natively without breaking the alpha layer, perfect for logos and overlay graphics.
makeSmaller.card2Desc: Maintain the pixel-perfect sharpness required for high-DPI displays and complex web interfaces.
makeSmaller.card3Desc: Efficient PNG sizes improve page load metrics and Core Web Vitals to help you rank higher.

why.title: Why Compress PNG?
why.p1: Unoptimized PNGs are often massive and can damage mobile loading speeds. By compressing your PNGs, you keep visual clarity and alpha transparency while saving bandwidth.
why.tipText: Add explicit width and height attributes to loaded PNGs on your site to prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.intro: PNG compression is lossless, meaning no visual quality is lost. The quality setting optimizes compression efficiency.
qualityGuide.maximumQualityDesc: Detailed graphics - Complex illustrations - Minimal compression
qualityGuide.recommendedDesc: Logos - UI icons - Best balance for most use cases
qualityGuide.highCompressionDesc: Web graphics - Thumbnails - Smaller file size priority
qualityGuide.maxCompressionDesc: Previews only - May show artifacts - Not recommended

items.question: Does PNG compression blur the image?
items.answer: No. PNG compression is typically lossless and focuses on removing redundant data while preserving pixel fidelity.

items.question: Should I use PNG or JPEG for my website?
items.answer: Use PNG for logos, interface elements, and transparency. For complex photos, JPEG or WebP usually gives better size reduction.

items.question: How does compressing PNGs improve my SEO?
items.answer: Smaller PNG files reduce page weight and improve loading metrics such as LCP, helping overall SEO performance.

items.question: Will the background stay transparent after compressing?
items.answer: Yes. The PNG alpha channel is preserved so transparent backgrounds remain intact.
